metric_name: Web browser vs DSS software per-seat cost
metric_value: Web browser: approximately $50/seat (quantity 1) vs. average $1,000/seat for traditional decision-support software
notes: Aberdeen's central economic argument for web-based BI; 20x cost reduction thesis

metric_value: NCR has approximately 12 years experience designing, managing and implementing data warehouses in virtually every industry
notes: Aberdeen's citation of NCR leadership position; Walmart as flagship example

metric_value: Traditional warehouse cost barriers: warehouse design + desktop software + hardware + connectivity + dedicated lines to suppliers/offices + replication costs as system grows
notes: Aberdeen's enumeration of up-front cost components that web eliminates or reduces

metric_name: Browser vs Windows client for power users
metric_value: Browsers lack OLE, cut-and-paste ease, formatting inheritance compared to Windows front ends; power users will initially need coexistence of client-server and web ROLAP
notes: Aberdeen's nuanced assessment; browser adequate for most users but not power users

metric_value: CGI is a single-threaded bottleneck for communicating from web server to applications; must be replaced by multi-threaded alternatives for enterprise BI
notes: Oracle WebSystem, Netscape, Microsoft all offering multi-threaded CGI alternatives

metric_value: Relational OLAP + HTML + Web server + cache-enhanced SQL = multidimensional analysis on the Web; MicroStrategy and Information Advantage leading implementations
notes: Aberdeen's description of first-generation web BI architecture

metric_value: Web DSS demands: scalability (incremental CPUs/RAID), high availability (online backup/redundancy), RDBMS parallelization, systems/network management; same as traditional warehouse but at higher scale
notes: Web amplifies demand for parallel-scalable server capabilities

metric_value: RDBMS is foundation for Relational OLAP and Web warehousing; parallel-scalable RDBMS required; proprietary multidimensional DBs incompatible with Web architecture
notes: Aberdeen's consistent advocacy for RDBMS-based architecture

metric_value: Security beyond data encryption/browser authentication required before enterprise Web DSS deployment
notes: Obstacle 1 of Aberdeen's 5-obstacle framework

metric_value: CGI single-threaded bottleneck must be replaced by multi-threaded alternative for enterprise-scale web BI
notes: Obstacle 2; Oracle, Netscape, Microsoft all addressing

metric_value: Browsers must mature to accommodate dynamic portfolio analysis, not just static financial data
notes: Obstacle 3; Aberdeen predicts resolution in 1-2 years

metric_name: Web DSS obstacle: limited app dev tools
metric_value: HTML-based development tools need to add pop-up windows and radio bars to match GUI desktop capabilities
notes: Obstacle 4; JavaScript/DHTML would address this

metric_value: Better integration with desktop applications (spreadsheets) required for power user adoption
notes: Obstacle 5; Excel web query and later Excel Online eventually solved this

metric_name: Intranet as first enterprise Web DSS opportunity
metric_value: Natural information-gathering, no-update affinity between decision support and Web makes DSS the first high-impact Web candidate
notes: Aberdeen's identification of DSS as killer app for enterprise intranet

metric_value: Aberdeen: as browsers mature, CEOs and high-level decision makers will mandate IS to build Web-enabled decision support
notes: Forward-looking executive demand prediction

metric_value: Web reapportions warehouse costs: savings on desktop software/deployment redirected to server hardware, RDBMS, and data preparation — exactly where investment belongs
notes: Aberdeen's thesis on optimal resource allocation in web-enabled BI era

metric_name: Decision support as OLTP pressure point
metric_value: Decision support is becoming so enterprise critical it is being placed in a reciprocal relationship with OLTP; IS must pay attention to OLTP-data download speed
notes: Aberdeen's prescient observation about real-time BI feeding back into transactional systems

metric_name: Web browser as universal BI thin client
metric_value: Aberdeen predicts Web browser will become the primary delivery mechanism for enterprise decision support, removing traditional DSS per-seat cost barrier
notes: Core prediction; pairs with OBS-019

metric_value: Web browser became universal BI client by 2000-2005; Tableau (2003), Power BI, Looker all browser-based; Salesforce CRM entirely browser-based; per-seat BI costs dropped dramatically
notes: Aberdeen's prediction confirmed fully; browser-based BI now dominant paradigm

metric_value: Aberdeen: most obstacles (security, CGI, browser maturity) will be swept away over next 1-2 years, driving new DSS/warehouse applications
notes: Pairs with OBS-021

metric_value: SSL security became standard by 1997; CGI replaced by ISAPI/NSAPI and FastCGI by 1997-1998; DHTML/JavaScript addressed browser limitations; all major obstacles resolved within Aberdeen's 1-2 year timeline
notes: Aberdeen's 1-2 year timeline proved accurate; obstacles resolved as predicted

metric_value: NCR Worldmark servers cover SMP, Clusters, and MPP configurations; Teradata scales from data marts to multi-terabyte detailed warehouses
notes: NCR as complete warehouse vendor: data mart to MPP enterprise warehouse

metric_value: NCR (infrastructure + consulting + industry templates) + MicroStrategy (ROLAP toolset) = 'warehouse-supplier synergy' model for enterprise Web DSS
notes: Aberdeen presents as exemplar partnership for enterprise web warehouse deployment

metric_value: Enterprise Web warehouse strategy: start small SMP, scale within SMP range if appropriate, or grow to MPP — same architecture at different scale without recompilation
notes: Aberdeen's prescient cloud-like elastic scaling vision

metric_value: Aberdeen: Internet will evolve into secure electronic backbone serving both intranet and business-to-business applications; decision support is the first high-impact candidate
notes: Prescient vision of internet as enterprise B2B data infrastructure
